div1.entryFree:
Discharge, subst. 1) volley: by d. of their artillery, H4A I, 1, 57. Used of sighs: As II, 1, 37. Troil. IV, 4, 43.
2) payment: will keep me here without d., money, or furniture, H6B I, 3, 172. of what's past, is, and to come, the d. Cymb. V, 4, 173.
3) dismission from service: thy soldiers have took their d. Lr. V, 3, 105.
4) performance, execution: an act whereof what's past is prologue, what to come in yours and my d. Tp. II, 1, 254.
